rdfs:label: PhysWorldLM Radar Instance Ontology (PWMO-INST)
rdfs:comment: Canonical worldwide radar instance database. Every entry is a real deployed or historically significant system, fully linked to PWMO taxonomy, component, RF signal-processing, and relationship ontologies.

AN_APG-77.rdfs:label: AN/APG-77
AN_APG-77.developmentHistory: Developed under the Advanced Tactical Fighter (ATF) program. First flight 1997, IOC 2005 with F-22A Block 20.
AN_APG-77.beamformingMethod: Analog/Digital hybrid; Digital beamforming in v1.5
strengths: Extremely low RCS of own platform synergy

AN_APG-81.rdfs:label: AN/APG-81
AN_APG-81.commonName: F-35 Lightning II Fire Control Radar

AN_SPY-1.rdfs:label: AN/SPY-1
AN_SPY-1.antennaType: Four fixed-face PESA arrays (90-deg coverage each)
limitations: Single transmitter per array face (PESA vulnerability)

AN_SPY-6.rdfs:label: AN/SPY-6(V)
AN_SPY-6.antennaType: AESA (modular Radar Modular Assembly RMA tiles)

AN_TPY-2.rdfs:label: AN/TPY-2
AN_TPY-2.commonName: THAAD Fire Control Radar / X-band BMD Radar

AN_FPS-132.rdfs:label: AN/FPS-132
operatorNations: USA (operated at Fylingdales UK, Thule Greenland, Beale AFB CA, Cape Cod MA, Cavalier ND)

AN_MPQ-65.rdfs:label: AN/MPQ-65
AN_MPQ-65.antennaType: Phased array with electronic elevation scanning; mechanical azimuth rotation
strengths: Combat proven (Gulf War, Ukraine, MBS intercepts)
limitations: Limited azimuth coverage in any snapshot
Known saturation vulnerability in massed attack

AN_TPS-80.rdfs:label: AN/TPS-80 G/ATOR
strengths: Multi-mission single radar replaces 4+ legacy systems

AN_TPQ-53.rdfs:label: AN/TPQ-53 Firefinder
AN_TPQ-53.scanMechanism: Mechanical azimuth rotation with electronic sector scan

E3_AWACS.rdfs:label: E-3 Sentry AWACS Radar
E3_AWACS.commonName: AWACS Airborne Warning and Control System Radar
E3_AWACS.operationalStatus: Active; being replaced by E-7A Wedgetail
E3_AWACS.antennaType: Slotted waveguide array in rotating rotodome

AN_APY-7_JSTARS.rdfs:label: AN/APY-7 JSTARS
strengths: Decisive in Desert Storm ground picture

Irbis-E.rdfs:label: Irbis-E (N035)
Irbis-E.antennaType: PESA with electromechanical two-axis tilt (+/-60 az, +/-60 el)
strengths: Mechanical tilt extends FOV beyond typical +/-60 deg

N036_Byelka.rdfs:label: N036 Byelka
N036_Byelka.description: Multi-aperture AESA system comprising N036-1 (nose main array), N036L-1-01 (port wing LE), N036L-1-02 (starboard wing LE), N036B-1-01 (rear-facing). Provides near-360 deg coverage.

LbandArrays.purpose: Counter-stealth; L-band wavelength approaches resonance for stealth aircraft
LbandArrays.capability: Degrades RAM effectiveness on X-band-optimized stealth aircraft

limitations: Su-57 fleet very small (~20 airframes as of 2025)

Nebo_M.rdfs:label: 55Zh6M Nebo-M
Nebo_M.description: Multi-band radar system combining four sub-radars (VHF+UHF+L+X) for counter-stealth, long-range detection, and high-resolution tracking.
Nebo_M.counterStealthCapability: VHF/UHF bands degrade effectiveness of X-band-optimized RAM; Nebo-M claimed to detect F-22/F-35 at tactically significant ranges

Voronezh_M.rdfs:label: 77Ya6 Voronezh-M
strengths: Coverage of all ICBM threat azimuths

Don_2N.rdfs:label: 5N20 Don-2N
Don_2N.commonName: Moscow A-135 ABM Battle Management Radar
Don_2N.description: Sole operational battle-management radar for Russia's A-135 Moskva nuclear-tipped ABM system protecting Moscow. Truncated pyramid structure houses four PESA faces.

Rezonans_NE.rdfs:label: Rezonans-NE
Rezonans_NE.counterStealthRationale: Resonance-region wavelength (60-200 cm) matches wingspan of stealth aircraft, degrading Rayleigh-region RAM and surface shaping absorption

S400_92N6.rdfs:label: 92N6E Grave Stone
S400_92N6.commonName: S-400 Triumph Engagement / Fire Control Radar
S400_92N6.antennaType: Phased array - electronic elevation, mechanical azimuth

Type_346A.rdfs:label: Type 346A
Type_346A.commonName: Dragon Eye Active Phased Array Naval Radar
Type_346A.description: China's primary carrier battle group escort radar. Type 346A on Type 055 is larger than 346 on Type 052D.
strengths: Comparable to AN/SPY-1D in mission scope
limitations: Technical details classified; performance estimates uncertain

KLJ_7A.rdfs:label: KLJ-7A
KLJ_7A.commonName: J-10C / J-16 AESA Fire Control Radar

JY_27A.rdfs:label: JY-27A
JY_27A.commonName: Chinese VHF Anti-Stealth Long Range Surveillance Radar

CAPTOR_E.rdfs:label: CAPTOR-E (E-Scan)
CAPTOR_E.manufacturer: Leonardo (UK), Indra (Spain), Hensoldt (Germany), Leonardo (Italy) - Euroradar consortium
CAPTOR_E.antennaType: AESA with beam-steerable CAESAR unit (+/-100 deg azimuth)
CAPTOR_E.specialFeature: CAESAR mechanically rotated AESA sub-array provides +/-100 deg continuous coverage - wider than fixed-nose AESAs
strengths: Wide angular coverage via rotating AESA

RBE2_AESA.rdfs:label: RBE2 AESA
RBE2_AESA.officialDesignation: RBE2-AA (Radar a Balayage Electronique 2 - Active Array)

SeaFire_500.rdfs:label: Sea Fire 500
SeaFire_500.commonName: FDI Frigate Multi-Function Naval AESA Radar

SMART_L.rdfs:label: SMART-L
SMART_L.commonName: Thales Long Range Naval Air Search Radar
aliases: SMART-L EWC (Early Warning Capability for BMD)
SMART_L.antennaType: Electronically scanned array on rotating mount

SMART_L_EWC.purpose: Horizon-extended BMD early warning using unique chirp compression and coherent integration

APAR.rdfs:label: APAR
APAR.commonName: DE Zeven Provincien / F125 Multi-Function Naval AESA
APAR.antennaType: Four fixed AESA faces (32,000 TRMs total across four faces)

SAMPSON.rdfs:label: SAMPSON MFR
SAMPSON.antennaType: Back-to-back rotating AESA pair (full 360 deg coverage via rotation)

Giraffe_4A.rdfs:label: Giraffe 4A
Giraffe_4A.commonName: Saab Medium Range Air Defense & Counter-UAS Radar

TRML_4D.rdfs:label: TRML-4D
TRML_4D.commonName: Hensoldt Counter-UAS / Short Range Air Defense Radar

Artisan_997.rdfs:label: Type 997 Artisan
Artisan_997.scanMechanism: Rotating antenna with electronic beam elevation

Erieye.rdfs:label: FSR-890 Erieye
Erieye.antennaType: Fixed PESA arrays (side-looking, both directions from dorsal radome)
Erieye.coverageBlindspot_deg: +/-30 deg nose/tail endfire due to fixed side-looking arrays

ELM_2052.rdfs:label: EL/M-2052
ELM_2052.commonName: IAI Elta AESA Multi-Mode Airborne Radar
modes: Air-to-ground: SAR, GMTI, DBS, Doppler-beam sharpening

ELM_2084.rdfs:label: EL/M-2084
ELM_2084.commonName: Elta Multi-Mission Radar (MMR) - Counter-Battery and Air Defense

MF_STAR.rdfs:label: MF-STAR
MF_STAR.commonName: Israel Navy Multi-Function Surveillance and Tracking AESA Radar

Uttam_AESA.rdfs:label: Uttam AESA
Uttam_AESA.officialDesignation: Uttam Active Electronically Scanned Array Radar
Uttam_AESA.commonName: Tejas Mk2 / LCA Navy Mk2 AESA Radar
Uttam_AESA.developmentHighlights: First indigenous Indian AESA radar. GaN wafers developed at GAETEC Hyderabad and SAMEER Mumbai. Full design authority retained by DRDO.

Swordfish_LRTR.rdfs:label: Swordfish Long Range Tracking Radar
Swordfish_LRTR.manufacturer: DRDO / LRDE (based on Green Pine technology transfer)
integratedC2: India BMD Phase-I (Prithvi Air Defense + Advanced Air Defense interceptors)

Arudhra.rdfs:label: Arudhra
Arudhra.commonName: IAF Medium Power Radar (MPR) - 3D medium range

Swathi_WLR.rdfs:label: Swathi Weapon Locating Radar
strengths: Proved in field trials under live fire

Rajendra_FCR.rdfs:label: Rajendra
guidanceModes: Track Via Missile (TVM) - simultaneous guidance of 4 missiles

Virupaksha_BFSR.rdfs:label: Virupaksha / BFSR-SR
Virupaksha_BFSR.commonName: Indian Battlefield Surveillance Radar - Short Range

AEW_C_DRDO.rdfs:label: DRDO AEW&C
AEW_C_DRDO.commonName: India Airborne Early Warning and Control System
AEW_C_DRDO.antennaType: AESA arrays (fore and aft fuselage - spine mounted)
AEW_C_DRDO.description: Indigenous AEW&C with spine-mounted dual-face AESA. No rotating rotodome - dual fixed arrays provide 240 deg coverage.
limitations: Coverage gap in 120 deg tail sector vs rotodome aircraft

J_APG_2.rdfs:label: J/APG-2
J_APG_2.commonName: JASDF F-2 AESA Fire Control Radar
strengths: First operational AESA outside USA (2000 timeframe)

J_FPS_5.rdfs:label: J/FPS-5
J_FPS_5.commonName: JASDF Ballistic Missile Defense Early Warning Radar

KF21_AESA.rdfs:label: KF-21 AESA Radar
KF21_AESA.commonName: KF-21 Boramae AESA Fire Control Radar
KF21_AESA.developmentNote: Indigenous KF-21 program with Israeli assistance (EL/M-2052 technology transfer elements). Full Korean AESA capability milestone.

Jindalee_OTHR.rdfs:label: Jindalee OTHR (JORN)
Jindalee_OTHR.description: Over-the-horizon radar exploiting ionospheric skywave propagation. Covers Australia's northern approaches with 3 networked sites.
strengths: Unique long range coverage of Australian EEZ and northern approaches
Detects surface and air contacts beyond any conventional radar

CEA_FAR.rdfs:label: CEA-FAR / CEAFAR
CEA_FAR.commonName: CEA Technologies Naval AESA Radar (RAN Hobart / Hunter class)
CEA_FAR.description: Unique modular scalable AESA design. CEAFAR (fixed panels) pairs with CEAMOUNT (fire control tracker). Indigenous Australian technology.

ASELSAN_GENIS.rdfs:label: ASELSAN GENIS BAND
ASELSAN_GENIS.description: Part of Turkey's MIFAR (Multi-Function AESA Radar) indigenous program. GaN-based, targeting TF-X Kaan 5th gen fighter integration.

Ukraine_Kolchuga.rdfs:label: Kolchuga-M
Ukraine_Kolchuga.commonName: Ukrainian Passive Electronic Surveillance / Coherent Location System
Ukraine_Kolchuga.operatingPrinciple: Passive coherent location: detects and tracks aircraft by their own RF emissions (radar, comms, IFF) using TDOA geolocation across four receiver sites
Ukraine_Kolchuga.LPI_resistance: Detects stealth aircraft that must still emit (comms, IFF) - does not require active radar reflection
strengths: Very long range on emitting targets
limitations: TDOA accuracy dependent on site separation

Poland_PET_PCL.rdfs:label: PET/PCL
Poland_PET_PCL.commonName: Polish Passive Radar System (Passive Coherent Location)

Norway_GLOBUS_III.rdfs:label: GLOBUS III
Norway_GLOBUS_III.description: Large AESA dish radar at Vardo, Norway. Contributes to US Space Surveillance Network (SSN). Also provides early warning for Russian ICBM testing.

Singapore_LSTAR.rdfs:label: LSTAR
Singapore_LSTAR.commonName: Singapore Light Surveillance and Target Acquisition Radar

Brazil_SABER_M60.rdfs:label: SABER M60
Brazil_SABER_M60.commonName: SAVIS / Omnisys Brazilian 3D Air Defense Radar

Iran_Bavar_373_Radar.rdfs:label: Meraj-4 / Sayyad Radar
Iran_Bavar_373_Radar.description: Indigenous Iranian long-range SAM radar analogous to S-300/S-400. Claims difficult to verify independently.

Sentinel_1_SAR.rdfs:label: Sentinel-1 SAR
Sentinel_1_SAR.applicationToStructDiff: Sentinel-1 IW SLC products are standard benchmark data for SAR despeckling research including DDPM/DDIM-based approaches (USF/StructDiff framework). Speckle follows complex circular Gaussian statistics in SLC; log-intensity for multilooked data.

RISAT_2BR.rdfs:label: RISAT-2BR1 / RISAT-2B
RISAT_2BR.militaryApplication: India's primary space-based ISR SAR for border monitoring (Pakistan/China LoC/LAC)
